verses.latin_vulgate: Psalmus Asaph.   Quam bonus Israël Deus,  his qui recto sunt corde !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִזְמ֗וֹר לְאָ֫סָ֥ף אַ֤ךְ ט֖וֹב לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֥ל אֱלֹהִ֗ים לְבָרֵ֥י לֵבָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Truly God is good to Israel, even to such as are of a clean heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mei autem pene moti sunt pedes,  pene effusi sunt gressus mei :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֗י כִּ֭מְעַט (נטוי) [נָטָ֣יוּ] רַגְלָ֑י כְּ֝אַ֗יִן (שפכה) [שֻׁפְּכ֥וּ] אֲשֻׁרָֽי׃
verses.king_james: But as for me, my feet were almost gone; my steps had well nigh slipped.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia zelavi super iniquos,  pacem peccatorum videns.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־קִ֭נֵּאתִי בַּהוֹלְלִ֑ים שְׁל֖וֹם רְשָׁעִ֣ים אֶרְאֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: For I was envious at the foolish, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia non est respectus morti eorum,  et firmamentum in plaga eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אֵ֖ין חַרְצֻבּ֥וֹת לְמוֹתָ֗ם וּבָרִ֥יא אוּלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: For there are no bands in their death: but their strength is firm.

verses.latin_vulgate: In labore hominum non sunt,  et cum hominibus non flagellabuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּעֲמַ֣ל אֱנ֣וֹשׁ אֵינֵ֑מוֹ וְעִם־אָ֝דָ֗ם לֹ֣א יְנֻגָּֽעוּ׃
verses.king_james: They are not in trouble as other men; neither are they plagued like other men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo tenuit eos superbia ;  operti sunt iniquitate et impietate sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֭כֵן עֲנָקַ֣תְמוֹ גַאֲוָ֑ה יַעֲטָף־שִׁ֝֗ית חָמָ֥ס לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore pride compasseth them about as a chain; violence covereth them as a garment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prodiit quasi ex adipe iniquitas eorum ;  transierunt in affectum cordis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָ֭צָא מֵחֵ֣לֶב עֵינֵ֑מוֹ עָ֝בְר֗וּ מַשְׂכִּיּ֥וֹת לֵבָֽב׃
verses.king_james: Their eyes stand out with fatness: they have more than heart could wish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cogitaverunt et locuti sunt nequitiam ;  iniquitatem in excelso locuti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָמִ֤יקוּ ׀ וִידַבְּר֣וּ בְרָ֣ע עֹ֑שֶׁק מִמָּר֥וֹם יְדַבֵּֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: They are corrupt, and speak wickedly concerning oppression: they speak loftily.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuerunt in cælum os suum,  et lingua eorum transivit in terra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שַׁתּ֣וּ בַשָּׁמַ֣יִם פִּיהֶ֑ם וּ֝לְשׁוֹנָ֗ם תִּהֲלַ֥ךְ בָּאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: They set their mouth against the heavens, and their tongue walketh through the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo convertetur populus meus hic,  et dies pleni invenientur in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֤ן ׀ (ישיב) [יָשׁ֣וּב] עַמּ֣וֹ הֲלֹ֑ם וּמֵ֥י מָ֝לֵ֗א יִמָּ֥צוּ לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore his people return hither: and waters of a full cup are wrung out to them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixerunt : Quomodo scit Deus,  et si est scientia in excelso ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽאָמְר֗וּ אֵיכָ֥ה יָדַֽע־אֵ֑ל וְיֵ֖שׁ דֵּעָ֣ה בְעֶלְיֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: And they say, How doth God know? and is there knowledge in the most High?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ipsi peccatores, et abundantes in sæculo  obtinuerunt divitias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּה־אֵ֥לֶּה רְשָׁעִ֑ים וְשַׁלְוֵ֥י ע֝וֹלָ֗ם הִשְׂגּוּ־חָֽיִל׃
verses.king_james: Behold, these are the ungodly, who prosper in the world; they increase in riches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixi : Ergo sine causa justificavi cor meum,  et lavi inter innocentes manus meas,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַךְ־רִ֭יק זִכִּ֣יתִי לְבָבִ֑י וָאֶרְחַ֖ץ בְּנִקָּי֣וֹן כַּפָּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Verily I have cleansed my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocency.

verses.latin_vulgate: et fui flagellatus tota die,  et castigatio mea in matutinis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֱהִ֣י נָ֭גוּעַ כׇּל־הַיּ֑וֹם וְ֝תוֹכַחְתִּ֗י לַבְּקָרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: For all the day long have I been plagued, and chastened every morning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si dicebam : Narrabo sic ;  ecce nationem filiorum tuorum reprobavi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אָ֭מַרְתִּי אֲסַפְּרָ֥ה כְמ֑וֹ הִנֵּ֤ה ד֖וֹר בָּנֶ֣יךָ בָגָֽדְתִּי׃
verses.king_james: If I say, I will speak thus; behold, I should offend against the generation of thy children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Existimabam ut cognoscerem hoc ;  labor est ante me :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָ֭אֲחַשְּׁבָה לָדַ֣עַת זֹ֑את עָמָ֖ל (היא) [ה֣וּא] בְעֵינָֽי׃
verses.king_james: When I thought to know this, it was too painful for me;

verses.latin_vulgate: donec intrem in sanctuarium Dei,  et intelligam in novissimis eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַד־אָ֭בוֹא אֶל־מִקְדְּשֵׁי־אֵ֑ל אָ֝בִ֗ינָה לְאַחֲרִיתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Until I went into the sanctuary of God; then understood I their end.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen propter dolos posuisti eis ;  dejecisti eos dum allevarentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֣ךְ בַּ֭חֲלָקוֹת תָּשִׁ֣ית לָ֑מוֹ הִ֝פַּלְתָּ֗ם לְמַשּׁוּאֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Surely thou didst set them in slippery places: thou castedst them down into destruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo facti sunt in desolationem ?  subito defecerunt : perierunt propter iniquitatem suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֤יךְ הָי֣וּ לְשַׁמָּ֣ה כְרָ֑גַע סָ֥פוּ תַ֝֗מּוּ מִן־בַּלָּהֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: How are they brought into desolation, as in a moment! they are utterly consumed with terrors.

verses.latin_vulgate: Velut somnium surgentium, Domine,  in civitate tua imaginem ipsorum ad nihilum rediges.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּחֲל֥וֹם מֵהָקִ֑יץ אֲ֝דֹנָ֗י בָּעִ֤יר ׀ צַלְמָ֬ם תִּבְזֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: As a dream when one awaketh; so, O Lord, when thou awakest, thou shalt despise their image.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia inflammatum est cor meum,  et renes mei commutati sunt ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֭י יִתְחַמֵּ֣ץ לְבָבִ֑י וְ֝כִלְיוֹתַ֗י אֶשְׁתּוֹנָֽן׃
verses.king_james: Thus my heart was grieved, and I was pricked in my reins.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ego ad nihilum redactus sum, et nescivi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִי־בַ֭עַר וְלֹ֣א אֵדָ֑ע בְּ֝הֵמ֗וֹת הָיִ֥יתִי עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: So foolish was I, and ignorant: I was as a beast before thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: ut jumentum factus sum apud te,  et ego semper tecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֣י תָמִ֣יד עִמָּ֑ךְ אָ֝חַ֗זְתָּ בְּיַד־יְמִינִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Nevertheless I am continually with thee: thou hast holden me by my right hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tenuisti manum dexteram meam,  et in voluntate tua deduxisti me,  et cum gloria suscepisti me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּעֲצָתְךָ֥ תַנְחֵ֑נִי וְ֝אַחַ֗ר כָּב֥וֹד תִּקָּחֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid enim mihi est in cælo ?  et a te quid volui super terram ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִי־לִ֥י בַשָּׁמָ֑יִם וְ֝עִמְּךָ֗ לֹא־חָפַ֥צְתִּי בָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Whom have I in heaven but thee? and there is none upon earth that I desire beside thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Defecit caro mea et cor meum ;  Deus cordis mei, et pars mea, Deus in æternum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כָּלָ֥ה שְׁאֵרִ֗י וּלְבָ֫בִ֥י צוּר־לְבָבִ֥י וְחֶלְקִ֗י אֱלֹהִ֥ים לְעוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia ecce qui elongant se a te peribunt ;  perdidisti omnes qui fornicantur abs te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־הִנֵּ֣ה רְחֵקֶ֣יךָ יֹאבֵ֑דוּ הִ֝צְמַ֗תָּה כׇּל־זוֹנֶ֥ה מִמֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: For, lo, they that are far from thee shall perish: thou hast destroyed all them that go a whoring from thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mihi autem adhærere Deo bonum est ;  ponere in Domino Deo spem meam :  ut annuntiem omnes prædicationes tuas  in portis filiæ Sion.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֤י ׀ קִ֥רְבַ֥ת אֱלֹהִ֗ים לִ֫י־ט֥וֹב שַׁתִּ֤י ׀ בַּאדֹנָ֣י יֱהֹוִ֣ה מַחְסִ֑י לְ֝סַפֵּ֗ר כׇּל־מַלְאֲכוֹתֶֽיךָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: But it is good for me to draw near to God: I have put my trust in the Lord God, that I may declare all thy works.
